[[Leon Pryce]] (cousin)
[[Will Pryce]] (2nd cousin) }} }} '''Waine Pryce''' (born {{birth date|1981|10|3|df=y}}) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg}}er and also as a {{rlp|ce}} in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at international level for [[England Knights|England 'A']] and [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]], and at club level for the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] (two spells) and the [[York City Knights]].{{cite web|url=http://jamaica-gleaner.com/gleaner/20110923/sports/sports7.html|title=Strong Squad Named For World Cup Qualifiers|publisher=jamaica-gleaner.com|date=31 December 2018|accessdate=1 January 2019}} ==Background== Waine Pryce was born in [[Bradford]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, he comes from a family of rugby league footballers with his father and uncles having played professionally, he has [[Jamaicans|Jamaican]] [[ancestor]]s, and eligible to play for [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] due to the [[Grandfather rule|grandparent rule]].{{citation needed|date=February 2019}} He is the cousin of the rugby league footballer; [[Leon Pryce]] and the rugby league footballer; [[Karl Pryce]].{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/9283022.waine-pryce-signs-a-deal-with-knights|title=Waine Pryce signs a deal with York City Knights|publisher=yorkpress.co.uk|date=31 December 2018|accessdate=1 January 2019}} As of 2014 he works as a mechanical engineer.{{cite web|url=https://twitter.com/wainepryce|title=Waine Pryce at twitter.com|via=Twitter|date=4 November 2018|accessdate=1 January 2019}} ==Castleford Tigers== Waine Pryce came through the Castleford Tigers Academy system as a youngster. He made his début for the senior team in 2000. He has scored 80 tries for CastlefordTigers in 140 starting appearances. During July 2006's [[Super League XI]], Pryce broke the [[tibia]] and [[fibula]] in his left leg away at [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]]. The extent of his injury forced him to have a metal rod and screws inserted into his injured leg.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/pryce-ready-to-resume-training|title=Pryce Ready To Resume Training|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|date=31 December 2018|accessdate=1 January 2019}} Because of the injury, Pryce missed the last two months of the 2006's [[Super League XI]], which saw Castleford Tigers relegated. For the 2007 Super League season, Pryce was transferred from the Castleford Tigers to the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats. Then in 2008 Pryce joined former Castleford Tigers teammate [[Tom Saxton]], and joined [[Featherstone Rovers]]. ==Wakefield Trinity Wildcats== Although relegated with Castleford Tigers from the Super League competition, Wakefield Trinity Wildcats signed Waine Pryce. Although Pryce missed the first few games of the season as he was still recovering from his injury. Waine has released by [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] as he never became a regular in the first-team. ==England== He has won international honours for the England 'A' side, making appearances against New Zealand, Tonga and Fiji. He has also represented England 'A' in the [[2003 European Nations Cup]] competition.{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=164|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120610184653/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=164|archivedate=10 June 2012}} ==Sources== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/winger-pryce-makes-hunslet-move/ Winger Pryce makes Hunslet move] *[https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/9748356.reserves-run-for-waine-pryce-as-he-eyes-knights-return/?ref=arc Reserves run for Waine Pryce as he eyes Knights return] *[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/dickens-miss-is-costly-for-rovers-1-2172462 Dickens' miss is costly for Rovers] *[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hunslet/hunslet-hawks-a-view-from-the-pitch-pryce-1-6009483 Hunslet Hawks: A view from the pitch - Pryce] *[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/boss-steals-a-march-on-york-1-2245310 Boss steals a March on York] *[https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/13499404.clare-aims-to-make-most-of-shock-move-to-bradford-bulls Clare aims to make most of 'shock' move to Bradford Bulls] {{DEFAULTSORT:Pryce, Waine}} [[Category:1981 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century British sportsmen]] [[Category:Black British sportsmen]] [[Category:Castleford Tigers players]] [[Category:English people of Jamaican descent]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Featherstone Rovers players]] [[Category:Hunslet R.L.F.C. players]] [[Category:Jamaica national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Jamaican rugby league players]] [[Category:Pryce family|Waine]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Bradford]] [[Category:Rugby league wingers]] [[Category:Sportspeople of Jamaican descent]] [[Category:Wakefield Trinity players]] [[Category:York Knights players]]
